etymology.description: semantic 金(金) (jīn, "metal") + phonetic 昔(昔) (xī, "past") -> grindstone made of metal -> wrong; mistake
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 金(金) (jīn, "metal"), indicating the concept related to metal, with 昔(昔) (xī, "past"), which provides the phonetic component. Originally, this described a grindstone, an implement for smoothing errors in metalwork, leading to the metaphorical use of 'wrong' or 'mistake' as it relates to correcting or being aware of mistakes.
etymology.simplification: The traditional component 釒(金) was simplified to 钅, retaining its representation of metal, which reduces the number of strokes.

examples.chinese: 我犯了一个严重的错误(我犯了一個嚴重的錯誤)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ fànle yī gè yánzhòng de cuòwù.

examples.chinese: 对不起，我说错了(對不起，我說錯了)。
这个答案是错的(這個答案是錯的)，请再检查一下(請再檢查一下)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè gè dá'àn shì cuò de, qǐng zài jiǎnchá yīxià.
examples.english: This answer is wrong, please check it again.
